event.question: Based only on the provided documents, what did Peloton Interactive disclose, announce, or report during this period?
event.headline: Peloton Interactive announces acquisition of Precor for $420 million, aiming to enhance U.S. manufacturing and R&D capabilities.
event.detail: On December 21, 2020, Peloton Interactive, Inc. issued a press release announcing the execution of the Purchase Agreement to acquire Precor for $420 million. The acquisition is expected to establish Peloton's U.S. manufacturing footprint, enhance research and development capabilities, and accelerate growth in commercial verticals. Precor President Rob Barker will become CEO of Precor and General Manager of Peloton Commercial, reporting to Peloton's President William Lynch.
doc_summaries: Peloton announces agreement to acquire Precor for $420M to establish U.S. manufacturing capacity
Press release detailing the Precor acquisition — U.S. manufacturing, R&D, and commercial-market expansion plans

Rob Barker, in his role as CEO of Precor and General Manager of Peloton Commercial, reports to William Lynch, the President of Peloton.
Hisao Kushi signed the Form 8-K report in his capacity as the Chief Legal Officer of Peloton Interactive, Inc. (Date/Time: 2020-12-21)

event.headline: Peloton reveals significant financial losses and internal control weaknesses in Q4 FY2021 results.
event.detail: On August 26, 2021, Peloton reported a net loss of $(313.2) million for Q4 FY2021, with total revenue growing 54% to $937 million and Connected Fitness Subscriptions reaching 2.33 million, a 114% year-over-year increase. The company identified a material weakness in internal controls over financial reporting related to inventory valuation, although it did not result in a material misstatement of financial statements. Peloton provided guidance for FY2022, projecting 2.47 million ending Connected Fitness Subscriptions and total revenue of $800 million.
doc_summaries: 8-K cover filing referencing FY2021 Q4 results and attached shareholder letter
Confident Q4 FY21 letter: announces new Peloton Output Park factory, sets aggressive FY2022 guidance ($5.4B revenue, 3.63M subscriptions)

Peloton provided FY2022 Q1 guidance projecting 2.47 million ending Connected Fitness Subscriptions, $800 million total revenue, gross margin of approximately 33%, and Adjusted EBITDA of $(285) million. (Date/Time: 2021-08-26)

event.headline: Peloton's leadership overhaul and restructuring plan signal a critical turning point amid financial challenges.
event.detail: On February 5, 2022, Peloton announced the resignation of longtime director Erik Blachford and the appointment of Barry McCarthy as CEO and President, with John Foley transitioning to Executive Chair. On February 8, 2022, the company revealed a comprehensive restructuring plan aimed at achieving at least $800 million in annual cost savings, which includes a workforce reduction of approximately 2,800 positions and a significant cut in planned capital expenditures.
doc_summaries: $800M annual cost savings, ~2,800 job cuts, winding down in-house manufacturing (POP), $150M capex cut
8-K cover referencing Q2 FY22 earnings and the restructuring press release
8-K detailing board changes and CEO transition, Foley to McCarthy, effective Feb 9 2022
Press release announcing new board directors; Barry McCarthy named CEO; John Foley becomes Executive Chair
Q2 FY22 letter: net loss $439.4M, guidance cut from $5.4B to $3.7-3.8B, Foley announces his own CEO transition, restructuring detailed

event.headline: Peloton Interactive announces CFO transition as Liz Coddington replaces Jill Woodworth amid leadership changes.
event.detail: On June 6, 2022, Peloton appointed Liz Coddington as Chief Financial Officer, effective June 13, 2022, succeeding Jill Woodworth, who is stepping down after serving since 2018. Coddington brings over 20 years of experience, including her most recent role as Vice President of Finance for Amazon Web Services. Woodworth will provide consulting services through September 13, 2022, to assist in reporting the company's fiscal year 2022 financial results.
doc_summaries: 8-K detailing CFO transition, Woodworth to Coddington
Press release announcing Liz Coddington as CFO, succeeding Jill Woodworth

relation_notes: Peloton Interactive, Inc. entered into a transition and consulting agreement with Jill Woodworth on June 6, 2022, under which she will provide consulting services through September 13, 2022, to support the reporting of fiscal year 2022 financial results. (Date/Time: 2022-06-06)

evidence.context: Liz Coddington was appointed as the Chief Financial Officer of Peloton Interactive, Inc., effective June 13, 2022. She brings over 20 years of experience in financial planning, analysis, and operational finance.

Under her offer letter dated June 6, 2022, Elizabeth Coddington is eligible for an annual base salary of $1,000,000, an equity award valued at $9,000,000 on the date of grant, and up to $150,000 in relocation reimbursement.

event.headline: Peloton Interactive announces global refinancing effort to repurchase existing notes and secure new credit facilities.
event.detail: On May 20, 2024, Peloton Interactive, Inc. announced its intention to launch a global refinancing effort, proposing to offer $275.0 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2029 and enter into a $1.0 billion five-year term loan facility and a $100.0 million five-year revolving credit facility. The company intends to use the net proceeds to repurchase approximately $800.0 million of its 0.00% convertible senior notes due 2026, refinance existing facilities, and pay related fees and expenses.
doc_summaries: 8-K announcing global refinancing: $275M convertible notes, $1.0B term loan, $100M revolver, repurchasing ~$800M existing notes
Press release detailing the refinancing terms
